heading: Declaration of policy
label: 485 Declaration of policy
text: For the purpose of providing for United States reclamation projects a feasible and comprehensive plan for an economical and equitable treatment of repayment problems and for variable payments of construction charges which can be met regularly and fully from year to year during periods of decline in agricultural income and unsatisfactory conditions of agriculture as well as during periods of prosperity and good prices for agricultural products, and which will protect adequately the financial interest of the United States in said projects, obligations to pay construction charges may be revised or undertaken pursuant to the provisions of this subchapter.
notes: References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , known as the Reclamation Project Act of 1939, which enacted this subchapter, sections 375a, 380a, and 387 to 389 of this title and section 16d of former Title 41, Public Contracts, and enacted provision set out as a note under . For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act Aug. 4, 1939, ch. 41853 Stat. 1187section 485j of this titlesection 485k of this title
